title: Professional Relationships, Community Participation, and Consent · Hiring Decision Lens: Follow Up Consent
summary: A learner treats networking as mass cold requests for referrals, extracts attention, ignores community norms, and measures people only by hiring power. Predict the hiring decision, evidence quality, affected people, uncertainty, and ethical boundary before reviewing a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the follow up consent increment in reciprocal relationship practice plan. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Record promised action, consented channel, timing, privacy boundary, thanks, and a respectful stop rule.
Contribute relevant help, documentation, testing, facilitation, learning notes, or community work within actual competence and norms.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# job-search cumulative learner-controlled career campaign
# Browser labs review fictional evidence contracts only. They never contact people,
# submit applications, access accounts, expose personal data, negotiate, or accept offers.
workspace: career
candidate-id: fictional-learner
target-role: [replace with role family, work outcomes, and bounded level]
jurisdiction: [replace with location and current official guidance boundary]
audience: [replace with reviewer need, time, format, and access context]
claim: [replace with one truthful role-relevant behavior claim]
evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, observation, changed case, contribution, and limit]
process-state: [replace with discover, investigate, apply, interview, offer, close, or archive]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
privacy-consent: [replace with purpose, minimization, permission, retention, deletion, and safe contact]
decision: [replace with learner-owned next decision and stop rule]
transfer-boundary: learner-approved external actions only
review:

steps.title: Predict Follow Up Consent · Theory Follow Up Consent
steps.instruction:
theory case — Automated messages ask strangers for referrals, ignore community norms and stop signals, and measure every relationship by immediate hiring access. The theory variation 6 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: defend one bounded career decision

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Follow Up Consent contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Predict Follow Up Consent · Theory Follow Up Consent.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for record promised action, consented channel, timing, privacy boundary, thanks, and a respectful stop rule. For Predict Follow Up Consent · Theory Follow Up Consent, keep Follow Up Consent connected to mechanically reviewable target-role, truthful-claim, contribution, accessibility, privacy, consent, process-state, changed-candidate, failure, repair, decision, and transfer evidence.

content.text: Automated messages ask strangers for referrals, ignore community norms and stop signals, and measure every relationship by immediate hiring access. The theory variation 6 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Record promised action, consented channel, timing, privacy boundary, thanks, and a respectful stop rule. Apply it to the concrete input and state the observable result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Name learner goal, target role, jurisdiction, audience, claim, contribution, source date, evidence revision, accessibility, privacy, consent, process state, changed candidate, rejected failure, causal repair, decision owner, non-claim, and transfer limit; then calibrate the application or career decision to that exact evidence. content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Automated messages ask strangers for referrals, ignore community norms and stop signals, and measure every relationship by immediate hiring access. The worked example variation 6 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Record promised action, consented channel, timing, privacy boundary, thanks, and a respectful stop rule. A defensible solution applies Follow Up Consent, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to reciprocal relationship practice plan.

content.code:
# Worked focus: career-follow-up-consent — Record promised action, consented channel, timing, privacy boundary, thanks, and a respectful stop rule.
workspace: career
candidate-id: fictional-candidate-2
focus: career-follow-up-consent
decision-evidence: Record promised action, consented channel, timing, privacy boundary, thanks, and a respectful stop rule.
target-role: accessible software quality engineer
jurisdiction: example location requiring current official review
audience: technical reviewer with ten minutes and keyboard-only access
claim: one bounded role behavior is supported by a revision-linked changed case
evidence: repository case study, exact test result, contribution note, and limitation
process-state: investigate before applying
accessibility: structured text, reflow, visible focus, plain-language status, and equivalent format
privacy-consent: publish no sensitive person or third-party data; contact only with explicit consent
decision: proceed only after identity, role, evidence, access, and risk checks pass
verify: inspect links, extraction order, revision, changed case, disclosure, and source date
failure: reject invented impact, hidden assistance, exposed data, or unverified recruiter identity
repair: correct the first unsupported claim and add a changed-candidate regression
owner: learner controls every external action
transfer-boundary: manual learner-approved profile, application, conversation, interview, and offer decisions
content.caption: Runnable transformation example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.
